text:
s: Preliminaries
There are some who hold that Primary or Seoondary direc
tions can rectify the time of birth. This needs a close examina
tion.
In the Primary directions we add one degree per year for
the years passed to the cusp of the tenth house and calculate
the chart. Some argue that we have to add 59'.8" per every
completed year only to the luminaries, ascendant and the
tenth house. The chart thus erected can reveal the d~?stiny, ac
cidents and the like for the year.
In the Secondary Directions note the number of years that
have elapsed and add the number to the Date of birth. Thus
the completed twenty six years for a person born on January
22, 1920 will come to Feb. 17, 1920.For the time of birth erect
the chart for that date. Compare it with the original and then
predict. If the events agree, then the time of birth given is
correct.
Note the aspects of the planets in these two forms of Direc
tion on the luminaries, lagna, and tenth house of the natal
chart.
